What We Monitor

Every motor-driven asset. One monitoring platform.

SAM4 monitors pumps, fans, compressors, mixers, conveyors, and any asset driven by an electric motor. If it draws current, we can monitor it.

Asset types we monitor

SAM4 covers the full range of rotating equipment in industrial plants. Here are the asset types we see most across our 10,000+ monitored assets.

Centrifugal pumps
Submersible, booster, borehole, process, injection, transfer, and circulation pumps. The most monitored asset type in our fleet.
Common detections Bearing degradation, cavitation, blockage, seal wear, imbalance, efficiency loss
Pump monitoring details →
Fans & blowers
Ventilation fans, cooling fans, process blowers, and aerators. Critical for safe atmospheric conditions in enclosed spaces.
Common detections Imbalance, bearing degradation, belt issues, blade fouling, process deviations
Fan monitoring details →
Conveyors
Belt conveyors, screw conveyors, and chain conveyors. Common in materials handling across mining, pulp, and manufacturing.
Common detections Bearing degradation, misalignment, belt/chain issues, blockage, overload
Conveyor monitoring details →
Agitators & mixers
Chemical reactors, blending vessels, and mixing tanks. Often submerged or enclosed, making visual inspection impossible.
Common detections Bearing degradation, seal failure, imbalance from product buildup, gearbox issues
Mixer monitoring details →
Compressors
Gas, air, and process compressors, including assets in ATEX/hazardous zones. High-value assets that are expensive to replace.
Common detections Bearing wear, valve faults, imbalance, efficiency degradation
Compressor monitoring details →
Archimedes screws
Screw pumps used in water treatment inlet works and low-head pumping stations. Critical for preventing pollution events.
Common detections Bearing degradation, trough wear, drive chain faults, efficiency loss
Screw pump monitoring details →
Electrical submersible pumps
Downhole ESPs in oil & gas production. Operate thousands of metres underground. Pulling the string costs days and hundreds of thousands of dollars.
Common detections Motor degradation, pump wear, gas lock
ESP monitoring details →
Canned motor pumps
Sealless pumps where the motor rotor is immersed in the process fluid. Impossible to access with vibration sensors.
Common detections Bearing degradation, rotor eccentricity, recirculation changes, winding faults
Canned pump monitoring details →
Transmissions
Gearboxes, couplings, belt drives, and chain drives. SAM4 detects faults across the full mechanical transmission path from motor to driven equipment.
Common detections Gear wear, belt/chain degradation, coupling faults, misalignment
Transmission monitoring details →
Low voltage motors
AC induction motors up to 1 kV: the backbone of industrial operations. SAM4 monitors the motor and the entire connected drivetrain.
Common detections Winding faults, bearing degradation, eccentricity, misalignment, supply issues
LV motor monitoring details →
Medium voltage motors
AC motors from 1 kV to 15 kV driving critical process equipment. High-value assets that are expensive to replace and hard to monitor with sensors.
Common detections Winding insulation degradation, rotor bar faults, partial discharge, bearing wear
MV motor monitoring details →
Asset not listed?
SAM4 monitors any AC motor-driven rotating asset. If it draws current, we can likely monitor it. Share your asset list and we'll confirm coverage.
Talk to us →

What we detect

ESA detects both electrical and mechanical faults through the motor current signature. One measurement point covers the full drive train.

Failure mode Component Detection type Typical lead time
Bearing degradation Bearing (motor or driven) Mechanical Weeks to months
Impeller wear / cavitation Pump hydraulics Process Days to weeks
Misalignment Coupling / shaft Mechanical Weeks
Stator winding insulation Motor electrical Electrical Weeks to months
Rotor bar defects Motor electrical Electrical Months
Belt / chain degradation Power transmission Mechanical Days to weeks
Gearbox wear Gearbox Mechanical Weeks to months
Dry running / seal failure Pump process Process Hours to days
Supply voltage imbalance Power supply Electrical Immediate
90.8%
Detection accuracy
0.32%
False communication rate
10,000+
Assets monitored
80+
Customers worldwide

0.32% false communication rate. Every alert goes through engineer validation before reaching your team. When SAM4 raises an alert, it means something. Your maintenance team trusts it because false alarms are nearly zero.

ESA vs vibration: coverage compared

Vibration monitoring requires a sensor on every asset. ESA reads the motor current from the MCC. covering the full drive train from one measurement point.

Full drive-train coverage
Vibration sensors see the bearing they're mounted on. ESA sees the motor, coupling, gearbox, and driven equipment through a single current measurement.
Submerged and enclosed assets
You can't mount a vibration sensor on a submerged pump or sealed mixer. ESA monitors from the MCC. no physical access to the asset needed.
Electrical faults included
Vibration can't detect stator winding degradation, rotor bar defects, or supply voltage issues. ESA detects electrical faults by nature.
Process anomalies visible
Cavitation, dry running, and load changes show up in the current signature. Vibration sensors typically miss process-related failures until mechanical damage occurs.

SAM4 detected possible bearing damage which was not picked up through vibration monitoring.

Royal Schiphol Group. Amsterdam Airport

See what SAM4 detects on your assets

Share your asset list. We'll show you which failure modes ESA covers and what detection lead times to expect.
